title: Ferrous magnetic materials
abstract: Soft magnetic materials may include a''-Fe 16 (P x Z 1-x ) 2 or a mixture of Î±''-Fe 16 P 2 and Î±"-Fe 16 Z 2 , where Z includes at least one of C, B, Al, Si, S, or O, and x is a number greater than zero and less than one. Techniques for forming soft magnetic materials are also described.
claims:

 Docket No.: 1008-365WO01/2023-285 10. The soft magnetic material of claim 9, wherein at least about 60 volume percent of the VRIW^PDJQHWLF^PDWHULDO^LV^WKH^DW^OHDVW^RQH^RI^WKH^Ä®Æ-Fe16(PxZ1-x)2 SKDVH^GRPDLQ^RU^WKH^Ä®Æ- Fe 8 (N x Z 1-x ) phase domain. 11. The soft magnetic material of claim 1, wherein the soft magnetic material is free of neodymium (Nd). 12. The soft magnetic material of claim 11, wherein the soft magnetic material is free of any rare-earth element. 13. The soft magnetic material of claim 1, further comprising a dopant. 14. The soft magnetic material of claim 13, wherein the dopant comprises at least one of Sc, Ti, V, Cr, Mn, Co, Ni, Cu, Zn, Zr, Nb, Mo, Ru, Rh, Pd, Ag, Cd, Pt, Au, Sm, C, Pb, W, Ga, Y, Mg, Hf, Ge, Al, or Ta. 15. The soft magnetic material of claim 1, further comprising a phase stabilizer. 16. The soft magnetic material of claim 15, wherein the phase stabilizer comprises at least one of B, Al, C, Si, O, Co, Cr, Mn, Ti, V, or S. 17. The soft magnetic material of claim 1, wherein the soft magnetic material has a saturation magnetization of at least about 200 emu/gram. 18. The soft magnetic material of claim 1, wherein the soft magnetic has a magnetic coercivity of less than or equal to about 10 Oersteds. 19. A soft magnetic material comprising a phospho-carbon-ferrous domain comprising a plurality of Fe 6 P clusters and a plurality of Fe 6 C clusters. 20. The soft magnetic material of claim 19, wherein each cluster of the plurality of Fe6P clusters comprises P at a geometric center of a respective octahedral arrangement of Fe, and wherein each cluster of the plurality of Fe6C clusters comprises C at a geometric center of a respective octahedral arrangement of Fe. 

 Docket No.: 1008-365WO01/2023-285 21. The soft magnetic material of claim 19, wherein the soft magnetic material is free of neodymium (Nd). 22. The soft magnetic material of claim 21, wherein the soft magnetic material is free of any rare-earth element. 23. The soft magnetic material of claim 19, further comprising a dopant. 24. The soft magnetic material of claim 23, wherein the dopant comprises at least one of Sc, Ti, V, Cr, Mn, Co, Ni, Cu, Zn, Zr, Nb, Mo, Ru, Rh, Pd, Ag, Cd, Pt, Au, Sm, C, Pb, W, Ga, Y, Mg, Hf, Ge, Al, or Ta. 25. The soft magnetic material of claim 19, further comprising a phase stabilizer. 26. The soft magnetic material of claim 25, wherein the phase stabilizer comprises at least one of B, Al, C, Si, O, Co, Cr, Mn, Ti, V, or S. 27. The soft magnetic material of claim 19, wherein the soft magnetic material has a saturation magnetization of at least about 200 emu/gram. 28. The soft magnetic material of claim 19, wherein the soft magnetic has a magnetic coercivity of less than or equal to about 10 Oersteds. 29. A method comprising forming the soft magnetic material of any one of claims 1 to 28. 30. The method of claim 29, wherein forming the soft magnetic material comprises physical vapor deposition from at least one of a C-doped, B-doped, Al-doped, Si-doped, S- doped, or O-doped iron source to form a layer comprising Fe, P, and at least one of C, B, Al, Si, S, or O over a substrate. 31. The method of claim 30, wherein the iron source comprises between about 1 atomic percent (at. %) and about 10 at. % of any of C, B, Al, Si, S, O, or P. 

 Docket No.: 1008-365WO01/2023-285 32. The method of claim 30, wherein physical vapor deposition comprises forming a phosphorus plasma to provide a source of P atoms. 33. The method of claim 32, further comprising annealing the layer at a temperature between about 100Â° C and about 210Â° C for between about 5 hours and about 100 hours. 34. The method of claim 29, wherein forming the soft magnetic material comprises depositing the soft magnetic material over a substrate using at least one of chemical vapor deposition, molecular beam epitaxy, low temperature epitaxy, liquid phase epitaxy, or ion implantation. 35. The method of claim 29, wherein forming the soft magnetic material comprises: straining an iron workpiece comprising at least one iron crystal in a direction substantially parallel to a <001> crystal axis of the iron crystal; phosphorizing and carbonizing the iron workpiece to form a phosphorized and carbonized iron wire or sheet; and annealing the phosphorized and carbonized iron workpiece to form the soft magnetic material. 36. The method of claim 29, wherein forming the soft magnetic material comprises: implanting P and C atoms in an iron workpiece using ion implantation; pre-annealing the iron workpiece to attach the iron workpiece to a substrate; and annealing the iron workpiece to form the soft magnetic material. 37. The method of claim 29, wherein forming the soft magnetic material comprises: heating an iron source to form a vapor comprising an Fe-containing compound; depositing Fe from the vapor comprising the Fe-containing compound, P from a vapor comprising a P-containing compound, and at least one of C, B, Al, Si, S, or O from a vapor comprising the compound containing the at least one of C, B, Al, Si, S, or O over a substrate to form a layer comprising Fe, P, and the at least one of C, B, Al, Si, S, or O; and annealing the layer comprising Fe, P, and the at least one of C, B, Al, Si, S, or O to form the soft magnetic material. 

 Docket No.: 1008-365WO01/2023-285 38. The method of claim 29, wherein forming the soft magnetic material comprises: heating an iron source to form a vapor comprising an Fe-containing compound; depositing Fe from the vapor comprising the Fe-containing compound and P from a vapor comprising a P-containing compound over a substrate to form a first layer comprising Fe and P; heating an iron source to form a vapor comprising an Fe-containing compound; depositing iron from the vapor comprising the Fe-containing compound and at least one of C, B, Al, Si, S, or O from a vapor comprising the at least one of C, B, Al, Si, S, or O over the substrate to form a second layer comprising iron and the at least one of C, B, Al, Si, S, or O; and annealing the first layer and the second layer to form the soft magnetic material. 39. The method of claim 29, wherein forming the soft magnetic material comprises: submerging a substrate in a coating solution comprising a P-containing solvent, an iron source, and a carbon source, wherein the coating solution is saturated with the iron source at a first temperature above a liquidus temperature of an Fe-C-P mixture to be deposited from the coating solution; cooling the coating solution to a second temperature to form a supersaturated coating solution, wherein the second temperature is below the liquidus temperature of the Fe-C-P mixture; maintaining the substrate in the supersaturated coating solution to allow a coating comprising Fe, C, and P to form over the substrate; and annealing the coating comprising Fe, C, and P to form the soft magnetic material. 40. The method of claim 29, wherein forming the soft magnetic material comprises: submerging a substrate in a first coating solution comprising a P-containing solvent and an iron source, wherein the first coating solution is saturated with the iron source at a first temperature above a liquidus temperature of an Fe-P mixture to be deposited from the first coating solution; cooling the first coating solution to a second temperature to form a supersaturated first coating solution, wherein the second temperature is below the liquidus temperature of the Fe- P mixture; maintaining the substrate in the supersaturated first coating solution to allow a first coating comprising Fe and P to form over the substrate; 

 Docket No.: 1008-365WO01/2023-285 submerging the substrate in a second coating solution comprising a solvent, an iron source, and a compound containing at least one of C, B, Al, Si, S, or O, wherein the second coating solution is saturated with the iron source at a third temperature above a liquidus temperature of a mixture to be deposited from the second coating solution; cooling the second coating solution to a fourth temperature to form a supersaturated second coating solution, wherein the fourth temperature is below the liquidus temperature of the mixture; maintaining the substrate in the supersaturated second coating solution to allow a second coating comprising Fe, P, and the at least one of C, B, Al, Si, S, or O to form over the substrate; annealing the first coating and the second coating to form the soft magnetic material. 41. The method of claim 29, wherein forming the soft magnetic material comprises: milling, in a bin of a milling apparatus, an Fe-containing raw material in the presence of a P source and a source of at least one of C, B, Al, Si, S, or O to generate a powder including Fe, P, and the at least one of C, B, Al, Si, S, or O; and annealing the powder to form the soft magnetic material. 42. The method of claim 29, wherein forming the soft magnetic material comprises: milling, in a bin of a milling apparatus, an Fe-containing raw material in the presence of a P source to generate a first powder including Fe and P; annealing the first powder to form at least one phase domain including Ä®^-Fe 16 P 2 or at OHDVW^RQH^SKDVH^GRPDLQ^LQFOXGLQJ^Ä®Æ-Fe 8 P; milling, in a bin of a milling apparatus, an Fe-containing raw material in the presence of a source including at least one of C, B, Al, Si, S, or O to generate a second powder including Fe and the at least one of C, B, Al, Si, S, or O; annealing the second powder to form at least one phase domain including Ä®^-Fe16P2 or DW^OHDVW^RQH^SKDVH^GRPDLQ^LQFOXGLQJ^Ä®Æ-Fe8Z, where Z includes the at least one of C, B, Al, Si, S, or O; and consolidating the first powder and the second powder to form the soft magnetic material. 

 Docket No.: 1008-365WO01/2023-285 43. The method of claim 29, wherein forming the magnetic material comprises: heating a mixture including iron and phosphorus to form a molten iron phosphorus- containing material; and solidifying the molten iron phosphorus-containing material to form a workpiece including at least one Fe16P2 phase domain. 44. The method of claim 29, wherein forming the magnetic material comprises: forming the soft magnetic material by a technique comprising melt spinning. 45. The method of claim 44, wherein forming the soft magnetic material by the technique comprising melt spinning comprises: forming a molten mixture comprising Fe and C; melt spinning the molten mixture comprising Fe and C to form Fe-C ribbons; and phosphorizing the iron-carbon ribbons to form an Fe-C-P material. 46. The method of claim 44, wherein forming the soft magnetic material by the technique comprising melt spinning comprises: forming a molten mixture comprising Fe and C; melt spinning the molten mixture comprising Fe and C to form Fe-C ribbons; ball milling the Fe-C ribbons to form Fe-C powder; and phosphorizing the Fe-C powder. 47. The method of claim 44, wherein forming the soft magnetic material by the technique comprising melt spinning comprises: forming a molten mixture comprising Fe; melt spinning the molten mixture comprising Fe to form Fe ribbons; and carburizing and phosphorizing the Fe ribbons to form an Fe-C-P material. 48. The method of claim 44, wherein forming the soft magnetic material by the technique comprising melt spinning comprises: forming a molten mixture comprising Fe and P; melt spinning the molten mixture comprising Fe and P to form Fe-P ribbons; and carburizing the Fe-P ribbons to form an Fe-C-P material. 

 Docket No.: 1008-365WO01/2023-285 49. The method of claim 44, wherein forming the soft magnetic material by the technique comprising melt spinning comprises: forming a molten mixture comprising Fe, C, and P; melt spinning the molten mixture comprising Fe, C, and P to form Fe-C-P ribbons; and phosphorizing the Fe-C-P ribbons to form an Fe-C-P material. 50. The method of claim 44, further comprising: assembling a plurality of the soft magnetic materials to form a bulk soft magnetic material. 51. The method of claim 29, wherein forming the soft magnetic material comprises: annealing a material including Fe, P, and at least one of C, B, Al, Si, S, or O in the presence of an applied magnetic field. 52. The method of claim 51, wherein the applied magnetic field has a strength of at least about 0.2 Tesla (T). 53. The method of claim 29, wherein forming the soft magnetic material comprises: compacting a plurality of nanoparticles comprising Fe 16 (PC) 2 or Fe 16 (PC). 54. The method of claim 29, wherein forming the magnetic material comprises: compacting a first composition comprising Fe, P and a second composition comprising at least one of C, B, Al, Si, S, or O to generate nanoparticles comprising Fe, P and at least one of C, B, Al, Si, S, or O by a chemical reaction process. 55. The method of claim 53, wherein an average maximum major dimension of the nanoparticles is in a range of 5 nm to 100 nm.
